style_prefix: Pen-and-watercolor illustration in the style of a contemporary illustrated ski guidebook. Confident black ink contour lines drawn with a fine pen describe every form, then filled with richly saturated watercolor washes — dense, layered, with visible brushstrokes and patches of unpainted white paper showing through within shapes. Visible cross-hatching and stippling in shadows. Crisp edges where ink meets paint. Detailed and storybook-illustrated, not loose or impressionistic. Palette: full-color but rooted in alpine blues, warm earth browns, deep evergreens, fresh snow whites, with a single saturated red accent where the subject calls for it. Center the subject in the frame; allow a thin white deckled paper border around the entire image. Do not exceed the borders of a single panel.

slots.prompt: Full-bleed watercolor: father standing in orange jacket, daughter fallen flat in the snow, alpine slope behind, other skiers in distance.
Watercolor: a single red Peak 2 Peak gondola cabin on a cable, descending past dark conifers. Isolated subject — let the cream paper ground breathe around it.
Watercolor portrait of a five-year-old daughter mid-mountain in pink jacket, distant skiers and conifers behind a wood-rail fence.

Character portrait, head-and-shoulders: The Vancouver Weekender. Middle-aged Canadian man leaning on an SUV plastered with three Whistler season-pass stickers. Smug, mid-explanation. Plain cream background.
Character portrait, head-and-shoulders: The Aussie Lifty (Jack). Young man, early 20s, sun-tanned, goggles on a black beanie. Working-holiday energy. Plain cream background.
Character portrait, head-and-shoulders: The Time-Share Heir. Older gentleman in a fur hat, faintly aristocratic, not dressed to ski. Plain cream background.
Character portrait: The Touring Couple in Arc'teryx. Serious couple in matching black technical shells, hauling loaded backcountry pulks. Plain cream background.
Character portrait, head-and-shoulders: The Vail Resorts Executive. Man in a navy suit holding a paper coffee cup, mildly out of place in a ski setting. Plain cream background.

Atmospheric watercolor panorama of Whistler valley at dusk — deep muted blues, silhouette mountain ridgelines, faint warm village lights in the lower valley. Wide horizontal composition with a calm, mostly-empty lower band intended to sit behind small text overlay. No text, no labels.

Top-down schematic ski-piste base map of Whistler Blackcomb resort, painted in pen and watercolor in the same style as the attached reference panel 5, BUT WITH NO TEXT AND NO ANNOTATIONS WHATSOEVER. Two shaded peaks (Blackcomb on the left, Whistler on the right) rendered in soft graphite-and-watercolor with snow-white tops and shadowed ridgelines. Suggested ski runs as faint, paler corridors winding down the mountainsides. Dark stippled conifer forest covers the lower flanks. A tiny painted village of small red-roofed buildings nestled at the bottom center. Cream paper ground, deckled paper border. Square composition. CRITICAL: absolutely no lettering, no labels, no text of any kind, no pin markers, no numbered circles, no dotted lines, no route arrows — all of those are added afterward as a digital overlay. The painted base must be entirely free of writing or graphic symbols. Just pure painted terrain.
Empty wide-open Symphony Bowl run, fresh snow, single ski track curving down.
Crystal Hut lodge alone on a snowy ridge, smoke from the chimney.
Plate of steak and fries with a cocktail glass beside it, warm interior light.
A pint of amber beer on an outdoor table in low afternoon sun.
A brewery-branded hat resting on top of brown paper shopping bags.
A single red Peak 2 Peak gondola cabin silhouetted against an alpine sunset.
